titulo: Decana de la Escuela de Ciencias Físicas y Nanotecnología
areas_investigacion: Decana de la Escuela de Ciencias Físicas y Nanotecnología
bio: Realizó el  Diploma, Maestría y Doctorado en el Imperial College (Universidad de Londres, UK) en Tecnología de Materiales y Ciencia de Materiales. Ha estado como profesor visitante en el Lawrence Berkeley Lab. Y  trabajo  como Investigador durante 2  años en la Universidad de Bristol en el Departamento de Física (H.H. Wills Physics Laboratory). Es jefe del Centro de Ing. Materiales y Nanotecnología  IVIC y profesor de la escuela de Física y de la Facultad de Ingeniería de la Universidad Central de Venezuela. Actualmente está como investigador Prometeo en el departamento de Física de la Escuela Politécnica Nacional hasta Octubre del presente año. Sus líneas de investigación se desarrollan en el área de síntesis y caracterización de nuevos materiales: Biomateriales, Nanomateriales y el estudio de su relación entre estructura y propiedades, especialmente con el uso de técnicas de difracción,  microscopia electrónica y fuerza atómica para la caracterización de estos materiales. Ha sido tutora de numerosas tesis de pregrado (50), Maestría (13) y Doctorado (11). Ha sido autor de 150 publicaciones internacionales, 63 trabajos en extenso y coautor de 586 presentaciones en Congresos,  entre  nacionales e internacionales,  Ha sido conferencista invitada en 119 eventos. Ha participado en la organización de alrededor de 30 eventos científicos Es editor jefe de la revista Acta Microscopica.   Editor asociado de la revista Microscopy & Microanálisis y de la Rev. de la Facultad de Ingenierí

titulo: Coordinador de la Maestría en Física Fundamental
bio: Soy Antonio Miguel Castillo Cofre, tengo 27 años y nací el 1 de diciembre de 1998. Realicé mis estudios de pregrado en la ciudad de Riobamba, en la Escuela Superior Politécnica de Chimborazo (ESPOCH), donde obtuve el título de Físico. Posteriormente, cursé mis estudios de posgrado en la Universidad de Investigación de Tecnología Experimental Yachay, alcanzando el grado de Magíster en Física Aplicada con mención en Nanotecnología. Mis intereses académicos y científicos abarcan el estudio de materiales avanzados, con énfasis en la investigación continua de nanomateriales, su síntesis, estructura y caracterización, particularmente en nanotubos de carbono, así como en el uso de técnicas experimentales para el análisis de sus propiedades físicas San Miguel de Urcuquí, Hacienda San José s/n Proyecto Yachay Teléfono: +593 6299 9500 Spanish Brochure Yachay Tech English Brochure Yachay Tech

titulo: Coordinador de la Maestría en Estrategias para la Docencia STEM
areas_investigacion: Coordinador de la Maestría en Estrategias para la Docencia STEM
bio: Estudió dos carreras simultáneamente: por un lado, realizó la licenciatura en Física-Matemáticas en la Universidad Técnica Particular de Loja adicionalmente, obtuvo la ingeniería en Finanzas en la Pontificia Universidad Católica del Ecuador. Posteriormente, realizó sus estudios de Maestría en Proyectos de Energías Renovables en la Universidad Europea Miguel de Cervantes, Valladolid, España. De igual forma, obtuvo el título de Magíster en enseñanza de la Física-Química en la Universidad Nacional de Educación a Distancia UNED en Madrid, España. Culminó su Doctorado en el Programa de Física Aplicada en la Universidade da Coruña en España, siendo el centro de su investigación los nanomateriales para la producción y almacenamiento de hidrógeno. Su experiencia en el ámbito educativo es muy diversa, desempeñándose como docente de educación superior y bachillerato. Trabajó en la Pontificia Universidad Católica del Ecuador durante cuatro años, laboró en la Universidad Técnica del Norte en la Facultad de Ciencias Agrícolas y Ambientales. Se desempeñó como Vicerrector Académico de la Unidad Educativa Víctor Manuel Guzmán, y posteriormente fue nombrado Rector encargado de la misma institución durante un año. Durante 5 años ha trabajado en la Universidad de Investigación de Tecnología Experimental Yachay, tiempo en el cual se ha desempeñado como docente de Física, Coordinador de Servicios Escolares, Coordinador de la Carrera de Educación en Ciencias Experimentales y Coordinador de práctic

Duncan Mowbray graduated with a Bachelor of Mathematics in Honours Applied Mathematics (with distinction) and a Bachelor of Science in Honours Physics (Dean’s Honours List) from the University of Waterloo in the year 2000.  He then received in 2003 a Master of Science in Physics from the University of Waterloo on “Coefficient Based Simulation of Quantum Algorithms”.  In 2007, Duncan was awarded a Doctorate of Philosophy in Applied Mathematics from the University of Waterloo for his research on “Hydrodynamic Modelling of Carbon Nanotubes”.  For the next two years, Duncan worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher in the group of Prof. Jens K. Nørskov at the Center of Atomic-scale Materials Design of the Technical University of Denmark.  Duncan then joined the Nano-Bio Spectroscopy Group and ETSF Scientific Development Center at the University of the Basque Country in 2010 under the supervision of Prof. Angel Rubio, where he was awarded the Juan de la Cierva Fellowship from the Spanish Ministry.   Finally, in 2016 Duncan joined the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology at Yachay Tech as an Associate Professor. Duncan has published 63 letters and articles, 19 as first author, in refereed journals such as Science, JACS, ACS Nano, JCTC, JPC Letters, Physical Review A and B. Theory development, modeling,  and simulation of nanomaterials, including the calculation of the structure, electronic properties, catalyic activity, and conductivity of novel nanomaterials, in the areas of p

bio: Henry Pinto obtained his PhD in Physics in the field of Condensed Matter Physics and Quantum Mechanical simulations at the Department of Physics at the National University of Ireland in Cork and Tyndall National Institute, Ireland, in 2006. During his PhD studies he was the winner of the BOC Postgraduate Bursary Award for his research. Through his scientific career, Dr. Pinto has been doing research in world class centers like: Aalto University, Finland; Nagoya University, Japan and Liverpool University, UK. From 2010 until 2016 Dr. Pinto was a Research Professor in the Interdisciplinary Center for Nanotoxicity at Jackson State University, USA. Dr. Pinto’s main scientific interests are in Condensed Matter Physics and Computational Materials Science applying cutting-edge theoretical and computational methods together with the best available computing resources. He has special interest in the understanding of the nanoworld phenomena from a theoretical perspective in close collaboration with experimentalist. His main research topics includes: electronic structure calculations, magnetism and strong correlated systems, mineral and metal-oxide surfaces and interfaces, polymers and metal/polymer interfaces, scanning tunneling microscopy simulations, nanomanipulation, molecular electronics, point defects and high performance computing. Current Research projects Effect of Interface Reconstruction on the Mechanical Properties of Al/Al2O3 Multilayers. Study of metal clusters/TiO2(110) i

bio: Clara Rojas was born in Valencia, Venezuela. She obtained her Physics degree at the Universidad de Carabobo in Valencia, Venezuela in 2001. Then she attended IVIC, Venezuela on a grant from FONACIT, and obtained his Ph.D in Physics in 2007. In 2008, she became a postdoc at IVIC for three years to reach in 2011 an Associate Research position at Center of Interdisciplinary Studies of Physics. Since September 2017 professor Clara Rojas is working in Yachay TECH University in the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology teaching several courses of theoretical physics and making research in Inflationary Cosmology and Relativistic Quantum Mechanics. Currently she is Full-Time Added Professor 1.

bio: Sarah Briceño, Ph.D., joined Yachay Tech in April 2017 as a Lecturer in the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology. She earned her Ph.D. in Applied Chemistry from the University of the Andes in Venezuela. After completing her Ph.D., Briceño accepted a Postdoctoral position as a Member of the Physics Condensed Matter Laboratory – IVIC, focusing on the Synthesis and Characterization of Magnetic Nanoparticles. She had previously been a Researcher at the Venezuelan Institute for Scientific Research IVIC – Venezuela. Optimization of the methods of synthesis of nanostructured materials for different industrial applications. bio: Werner Bramer Escamilla was born in Valencia, Venezuela. He obtain his undergraduate degree at the Univesidad de Carabobo in 2001. Then he obtain his Ph.D at the Instituto Venezolano de Investigaciones Cientificas in 2008. Assistant Professor, Hired to teach the course of Electromagnetism in the Departamento de Matemáticas y Física del Instituto Pedagógico de Caracas – UPEL. In 2008, he became a postdoc at IVIC for three years to reach in 2012 an Associate Research position at the Physics Center on the Condensed Matter Laboraty. He also have taught postgrad courses at the Physics center of IVIC. Since September 2017 he develops his teaching and research career at the School of Physical Sciences and Technology in Yachay TECH University.
publicaciones: CNB Espinosa, C Sabater, C Untiedt, E Medina, W Bramer, “Conductance quantization in atomic-sized gold contacts using a low cost mechanical controllable break junction setup” European Journal of Physics (2020)

bio: Miguel Ángel Suárez Ledo es un físico con amplia experiencia en docencia, especializado en computación científica. Licenciado en Física por la Universidad Central de Venezuela y con una maestría en la misma institución, ha desarrollado una dilatada carrera académica en universidades de Venezuela y Ecuador. Actualmente, es profesor titular auxiliar en la Universidad de Investigación de Tecnología Experimental Yachay (UITEY), donde también ha desempeñado roles administrativos. Sus áreas de interés incluyen la modelización computacional, física de sistemas complejos y enseñanza de la programación aplicada a las ciencias.
publicaciones: “Ciencias e Ingeniería para estudiantes de Python”. 2015. ISBN: 978-980-12-9190-9.
“Out-of-equilibrium dynamics of a fractal model gel”. J. Chem. Phys., 130:194904, 2009. DOI: 10.1063/1.3129247

bio: PhD in Physics, University of Texas at Austin, U.S.A. (1990). Postdoctoral Research Fellow, University of Toronto, Canada (1990-1992). Master in Physics, University of California, Santa Barbara, U.S.A. (1984). Licenciado in Physics, Universidad Central de Venezuela, Caracas (1980). Full Professor, Universidad de Los Andes, Mérida, Venezuela (1993-2018). Senior Associate of the International Center for Theoretical Physics, Trieste, Italy (2012-2016). Associate Member, ICTP-South American Institute for Fundamental Research, Sao Paulo, Brazil. Since 2018, Professor and Director of the research group “Grupo Interdisciplinario de Sistemas Complejos” at Universidad Yachay Tech, Ecuador. Supervisor of dozens of PhD, MSc, and Bachelor thesis. Director of many research projects. Invited Professor and Visiting Scientist at universities and research centers in various countries. Invited speaker at several international conferences. Member of organizing committees of international conferences. Academic awards include: Magna Cum Laude, Universidad Central de Venezuela; Welch Foundation Fellowship, University of Texas, USA; Regional Prize for Science and Technology, Ministry of Science, Venezuela; Medal for Academic Merit, Association of Professors, Universidad de Los Andes, Venezuela; Prize for Best Scientific Article, Fundacite-Mérida; Prize for Best Research Group, Fundacite-Mérida; Physics Prize from the Academy of Physical, Mathematical and Natural Sciences of Venezuela; Polar Foundat

I have been focused over the past years on scientific research in the Nanotechnology field driven by my PhD studies. Born in Latacunga Ecuador, studied at National Polytechnic School (EPN) starting in Electrical Engineering and moved later to the Science School to been graduated as a physicist (2007),  later on he made a Master in Communications Networks and Sensors at the Pontifical Catholic University (2013), followed by doctoral studies in the University of Vienna in Austria and graduated with distinction (Sehr gut) 2018. His work has been related with the academia at Army University (ESPE), Salesian Polytechnic School, Technical University of Cotopaxi and University of Vienna. Also in a non academic field he was part of the young entrepreneur leaders of the Junior Chamber International (JCI) in Ecuador to taking action and creating good impact in our communities for I continue to follow their activities. Several times awarded with trainee grants from ICTP (Italy) 2009,10,11,12, Ministry of commerce of Popular Republic of China (2010), Visitor Scientist at Tokyo Metropolitan University in Japan (2014, 2015). I am a physicist with great interest in both new materials and applied subjects in electronics. Selected publications Very high boron-doping on single-walled carbon nanotubes from a solid precursor. bio: Darwin Méndez Jiménez was born in Cayambe, Ecuador. He received his degree in Mechanical Engineering from the Universidad Politécnica Salesiana (UPS) in 2017, while at the same time he worked in preventive and corrective maintenance tasks in the dairy and food industries, as well as in the construction of machines and mechanical parts for agricultural machinery. (from 2010 to 2018) Darwin works in Yachay Tech in the Faculty of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology since 2018 in the workshop in the constructive part of the projects of the students of the university. Julio Chacon obtained his B.Sc. in Mechatronics Engineering at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Mexico in 2008. He enrolled the graduated program of Applied Sciences at the Institute of Science and Technology of San Luis Potosi in Mexico and further moved to Vienna in 2010 where he conducted his PhD within a French-Austrian Cooperation program. During his PhD he was funded by the Austrian Science Foundation (FWF) and the OEAD Amadeus Program. He graduated in November 2013 with a thesis entitled: "Theory and Spectroscopy on functionalized graphene and GICs”. In May 2014 he was awarded a DRS Posdoctoral Fellowship at Freie Universität Berlin, Germany, and spent two years and a half in Berlin working on a project related to in-situ graphene and carbon nanotubes doping and characterization under inert and high-vacuum conditions. In September 2016, Julio joined Yachay Tech University as an Assistant Professor where he is the heard of the Nanotechnology department in the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology, the Coordinator of the Master degree in Applied Physics, and the International relations representative from the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology.
publicaciones: Julio has currently a set of (19) Peer review publications: (2) JACS (1) Appl. Phys. Lett. (2) ACS Nano (1) Carbon (1) Angew. Chem. (1) Phys. Rev. B (2) Scientific reports (7) Phys. Status Solidi B with 638 citations and h-index: 12 (Google scholar)

bio: David Andrade graduated with a Bachelor in Physics from the Physics Department of the National Polytechnic School in the year 2014. After that, David worked as a researcher in the National Institute of Meteorology and Hydrology (INAMHI) and the National Institute of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energies (INER). In 2015 David joined the Sciences Faculty at National Polytechnic School as a junior lecturer. In 2018 he was awarded a full scholarship to study for a master’s, and in 2019 he received a Master of Science in Theoretical Physics from the Mathematics Department at King’s College London in the UK on “Supergravity and Branes.”. David then joined the Universidad Nacional de Chimborazo as an occasional professor. Finally, in 2021, David joined the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology at Yachay Tech as an Assistant Professor. Currently, David researches in the field of theoretical astrophysics using GR to find theoretical solutions for compact objects and in the study of black holes.
publicaciones: – Arévalo, V.A., Andrade, D. & Rojas, C. Time evolution of the Von Neumann entropy for a Kerr–Taub–NUT black hole. Eur. Phys. J. C 84, 932 (2024). https://doi.org/10.1140/epjc/s10052-024-13290-8

bio: Joselin E. Guananga Gavilanez, was born in the city of Riobamba, Ecuador. He obtained the degree in Physics at the Escuela Superior Politecnica de Chimborazo (ESPOCH), Ecuador 2022. Her thesis was based on the Analysis of the incidence of a radioactive source in a bipolar scalpel for resection of carcinomas in ex vivo tissue, published as a scientific article as first author, in the Alfa Publicaciones magazine. In the same year, 2022, he began the postgraduate program in Applied Physics with a mention in Nanotechnology at the Yachay Experimental Technology Research University (Yachat Tech) and decided to move to the city of Ibarra, Ecuador. Where he began his work as Teaching Technician 1 in the unit of the School of Physical Sciences and Nanotechnology of the same university.
publicaciones: Analysis of the incidence of a radioactive source in a bipolar scalpel for resection of carcinomas in ex vivo tissue. DOI: https://doi.org/10.33262/ap.v4i3.230

bio: Pablo Neptalí Escobar Tibán, born in the city of Puyo, Ecuador. Physicist graduated from the Escuela Superior Politécnica de Chimborazo (2021); Master in Applied Statistics at the Universidad Politécnica Estatal del Carchi (2023). He is in continuous training receiving courses related to topics of his interest such as Physics, Astrophysics, Mathematics, Statistics, Pedagogy. His direct work as a teacher in Higher Education Institutions to date has focused on being a teacher at the “Universidad Nacional de Educación” in the program of Teacher Professionalization of the Amazon dictating the subject of Research Methodology, he has also taught the subject of Mathematics at the level of Higher Basic and High School, as well as the subject of Physics being a teacher in the Unidad Educativa “San Vicente Ferrer”. He has been linked to the Feynmania Institution, which is a group of professionals dedicated to private and personal teaching in different areas such as: Physics, Mathematics, Statistics. He considers himself a proactive person, who cares about helping students to develop and shape their study path.

Benjamín A Pusay Villarroel es Ph.D. por el Departamento de Ingeniería Electrónica de la Universidad Politécnica de Cataluña – España. Máster Universitario en Ingeniería de las Telecomunicaciones e Ingeniero Electrónico por la Università degli studi della Calabria en Rende – Italia. Investigador con más de cuatro años de experiencia en el grupo de Micro y Nanotecnologías de la UPC- Barcelona con experiencia en diseño electrónico, fabricación y caracterización de dispositivos electrónicos y materiales semiconductores. Experiencia en síntesis de materiales con técnicas modernas como Sputerring, PECVD, ALD, CVD, PVD, CBD. Desde el 2023, es Profesor Ocasional 1 en la Escuela de Ciencias Físicas y Nanotecnología. El campo de investigación actual se basa en la síntesis de materiales y precursores para aplicaciones en nanotecnología en el grupo de investigación GIAANT. María Fernanda Bósquez-Cáceres nació en Guaranda, Ecuador. Recibió su título de grado en Química en la Universidad Yachay Tech, con la Mención Honorífica Magna Cum Laude en 2023.  Realizó su proyecto de integración curricular enfocado en el diseño de materiales basados en biopolímeros como electrolitos para baterías de zinc-aire. En este campo, publicó tres artículos científicos que conformaron su tesis, además de un artículo como coautora. Desde octubre de 2023 trabaja en la Escuela de Ciencias Físicas y Nanotecnología como Técnico de Laboratorio. Además, forma parte del programa de Maestría en Ciencias Químicas con Mención en Ciencia e Ingeniería de los Materiales en la Universidad Yachay Tech. Soy Gerardo Acosta, ingeniero en mecatrónica de profesión, con una certificación internacional en fabricación digital, prototipado y tecnologías emergentes, con más de 8 años de experiencia en el desarrollo y gestión de laboratorios de innovación. Actualmente, lidero la administración del laboratorio FabLab Yachay Tech, un espacio orientado a la investigación experimental y la educación STEM, donde la academia y la tecnología convergen para impulsar soluciones innovadoras. Mi formación incluye un Master en Administración y Dirección de Empresas (MBA) y actualmente me encuentro cursando un Master en Ingeniería Biomédica. He trabajado en proyectos de desarrollo tecnológico dentro de áreas de la fabricación aditiva y sustractiva con orientación al prototipado rápido avanzado, además con experiencia para desempeñar procesos de mentoría para emprendedores y orientación en modelos de negocio. Mi misión es convertir el FabLab en un núcleo de innovación y experimentación científica, brindando herramientas y metodologías que potencien el desarrollo de proyectos interdisciplinarios, además de consolidarlo como un referente en investigación experimental, fabricación digital y transferencia de conocimiento, impulsando a estudiantes, docentes, investigadores y emprendedores a materializar ideas que transformen el futuro. fablab, maker, educación, prorotipo, investigación, desarrollo, innovación, creatividad San Miguel de Urcuquí, Hacienda San José s/n Proyecto Yachay Teléfono: +593 6299 9

Soy Antonio Miguel Castillo Cofre, tengo 27 años y nací el 1 de diciembre de 1998. Realicé mis estudios de pregrado en la ciudad de Riobamba, en la Escuela Superior Politécnica de Chimborazo (ESPOCH), donde obtuve el título de Físico. Posteriormente, cursé mis estudios de posgrado en la Universidad de Investigación de Tecnología Experimental Yachay, alcanzando el grado de Magíster en Física Aplicada con mención en Nanotecnología. Mis intereses académicos y científicos abarcan el estudio de materiales avanzados, con énfasis en la investigación continua de nanomateriales, su síntesis, estructura y caracterización, particularmente en nanotubos de carbono, así como en el uso de técnicas experimentales para el análisis de sus propiedades físicas San Miguel de Urcuquí, Hacienda San José s/n Proyecto Yachay Teléfono: +593 6299 9500 Spanish Brochure Yachay Tech English Brochure Yachay Tech
